---

## Version 2026.3

This release includes some big improvements to the [standalone frontend](/experimental/react-front-end),
upgrades to Vite 8, and includes some minor fixes.

### Standalone Frontend Improvements

The [standalone frontend](/experimental/react-front-end) continues to improve,
getting closer to feature parity with the standard Django front end.

This release adds many user and authentication features and team management support for team-based builds.

#### Profile Page

The profile page has been rebuilt with improved account management capabilities.

Details:

- **Added email address management** — add, remove, verify, and set primary email addresses.
- **Added connected accounts management** — connect and disconnect social accounts.
- **Added two-factor authentication setup** — enable/disable TOTP-based 2FA, scan QR codes, and manage recovery codes.
- Improved the profile page layout with card-based sections for a cleaner UI built on shadcn.

#### Teams Management

Teams can now be managed from the React frontend:

- **Added a Team Settings page** to the dashboard with team name editing, member list, invitation management, and a danger zone for deleting teams.
- **Added a Team Switcher component** to the dashboard sidebar for switching between teams and creating new teams.
- **Added a `TeamProvider` context** that makes the active team available throughout the React app.

#### Other Frontend Changes

- **Improved auth page forms** to use proper `<form>` elements with `onSubmit` handlers instead of button click handlers,
  improving keyboard accessibility and UX (Enter key to submit).
- Updated various auth pages (social auth callback, provider signup, email verification, MFA) to use
  consistent shadcn/ui components (`Button`, `Input`, `Label`) instead of DaisyUI class-based styling.
- Added CSS theme tokens for `success` and `warning` colors, based off the existing DaisyUI theme colors.
- Migrated from individual `@radix-ui/*` packages to the unified `radix-ui` package.

### Vite 8 support

The main front end and the standalone frontend now both use Vite 8.
This simplifies the front end build process and should drastically improve performance building production assets.

See the upgrading section for details.


### Other Changes

- **Upgraded most JavaScript packages to their latest versions**.
- **Switch to using the `tailwindcss/vite` plugin instead of postcss for Tailwind CSS in the Django front end.**
  - Also dropped the no-longer used `postcss.config.js` file.
- Added the ability to resend team invitations via a new API endpoint. This was to support the standalone front end feature.
  - Also added basic tests for this API.
- Made minor improvements to the existing React teams code:
  - Use relative URLs instead of absolute URLs for internal links.
  - Make it possible to pass a urlBase prop to the components.
  - Make it possible to pass image paths to the components.
  - Explicitly state that `gettext` is assumed to be a global when used.
- Added a shared `i18n.js` utility for translation functions (`gettext`, `interpolate`)
  in preparation for introducing internationalization support in the future.
- Switched from `psycopg2` to `psycopg` (psycopg 3) in development and `psycopg[c]` in production. Thanks Brennon for the suggestion!
  The production Dockerfile now installs `libpq-dev` and `gcc` for compiling the C extension.
- Fixed `bootstrap_subscriptions` management command for dj-stripe 2.10+ compatibility.
  The command now syncs the Stripe Account before syncing products, preventing "Account matching query does not exist" errors.
  Thanks Julian for reporting!
  - Also switched the command to use `self.stdout.write` instead of `print`.
- Upgrade sentry to the latest version and re-enable the Pydantic AI sentry integration, now that
  [https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-python/pull/5522](https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-python/pull/5522) is fixed.
- Added `legacy-peer-deps=true` to `.npmrc` files because `@tailwindcss/vite` (4.2.1) only declares peer support for Vite 5/6/7, not Vite 8.
  This will be removed once Tailwind releases a version that supports Vite 8 in its peer dependencies.

### Upgrading

See the [Vite 8 migration guide](https://vite.dev/guide/migration) for details on upgrading your project to Vite 8.
If you haven't made heavy customizations to your front end build process, this should be a straightforward upgrade
and you should not need to change anything on top of what Pegasus does automatically.

The psycopg change should work seamlessly with supported Pegasus deployments.
If you have a non-standard/manual set up, you may need to install the prerequisites for building the psycopg C extension.

For example, on Ubuntu:

```bash
sudo apt install clang build-essential libpq-dev python3-dev
```

Alternatively, you can change `psycopg[c]` to `psycopg[binary]` in your `pyproject.toml` file which should not require any additional setup.

*March 17, 2026*
